# Sales trip planner panel development

**User**

@total-hemp-consumables 
Sales Trip Planner Panel – Design & Implementation

Overview

We will create a Medusa Admin custom panel for planning a sales trip itinerary. This panel will allow a user to input trip parameters (addresses, time window, required stops, etc.), and it will produce an optimized route plan with both required and optional stops. The solution integrates Google Maps Platform APIs to find THCa/CBD store candidates along the route and compute efficient driving directions. The emphasis is on a robust, easy-to-use UI (since the trip is imminent), and on leveraging Google’s routing services for accurate travel times and stop optimization. Below we outline the UI components, backend logic, and output generation in detail.

UI Design and Inputs

The admin panel page will be a single-page form interface where the user can enter all trip planning inputs in a logical flow:
	•	Start and End Address – Two address input fields for the trip’s starting point and final destination. We will use text inputs (ideally with address auto-complete to reduce errors). A “Round Trip” checkbox is provided; if checked, it automatically copies the Start address into the End address field (making the trip end where it began).
	•	Must-Visit Stores List – A dynamic list component for entering required stop addresses. The user can click an “Add Store” button to append new address fields. Each store entry includes:
	•	An Address input (text or autocomplete).
	•	A Service Time input (defaulting to 30 minutes), allowing the user to edit the expected stop duration at that location. This ensures we account for meeting/service time at each must-visit stop.
	•	Trip Time Window – Two time-picker fields (or dropdowns) labeled “Start Time” and “End Time.” These define the allowable time range for the trip (e.g. depart no earlier than 8:00 AM and finish by 5:00 PM). The UI will likely use hour/minute selectors or a time picker widget for ease of use. This time window is crucial for fitting optional stops.
	•	Max Optional Stops – A dropdown or number selector for the maximum number of optional stops (THCa/CBD stores) the user wants to include (e.g. 0, 1, 2, … up to some reasonable limit like 5 or 10). This lets the user cap how many extra stops to attempt, to avoid an overly long itinerary.
	•	Action Button – A prominent “Plan Trip” (or “Generate Itinerary”) button at the bottom of the form. Once clicked, it will trigger the backend logic to calculate the route and itinerary.

The UI will be built within the Medusa Admin dashboard (likely as a React component). Medusa allows adding custom admin routes or widgets, so we’ll register this panel under an appropriate section (e.g. “Routes” or “Trip Planner”) in the admin. The form will include basic validation (e.g. ensure addresses are not empty, start time is before end time, etc.). Overall, the layout will be clean and intuitive, using concise labels and placeholders (e.g. “Enter address”) to guide the user. Short helper texts might be included (for example, under Optional Stops dropdown: “THCa/CBD stores along route to include (max).”). The design goal is to have all inputs visible in one view without excessive scrolling, given it’s a focused tool.

Backend Trip Planning Logic

Clicking “Plan Trip” will initiate a sequence of backend operations. We will implement a Medusa backend endpoint (or use a plugin) to handle the heavy lifting using Google’s APIs. The high-level process:

1. Route Calculation with Must-Visit Stops

First, we compute the base route that includes all must-visit addresses. We will use the Google Maps Routes API (the modern Directions service) for this step. The start address, each must-stop, and the end address form the waypoints of this route. We can call the computeRoutes endpoint with these points in order. This returns detailed route data including the encoded polyline for the path and travel time/distance for each leg. We ensure to request an encoded polyline in the response ￼, because that polyline will be needed to search for stops along the route. If the user did not specify an order for must-stops (i.e. they just provided a list), we could either honor the input order or ask Google to optimize their order. The Google Routes API supports an optimizeWaypointOrder flag to reorder waypoints for shortest travel ￼. For this first version, we might assume the user’s input order is the intended order (for simplicity and predictability). If needed, enabling optimization would yield a re-ordered sequence of the must-stops for a shorter route (the API can return the optimized order indices ￼).

We also account for the service durations at each must-stop. The Routes API itself will return just travel times; we will later add the 30-minute service time to each must-stop’s schedule when building the itinerary timeline. If a departure time (start of trip) is provided, we can pass it to the Routes API so that travel times (ETAs) consider traffic at that time for accuracy. The output of this step is a base route including: the ordered list of stops (start, must-visits, end), the encoded route polyline, and travel time for each segment between stops.

2. Discovering Optional THCa/CBD Stops Along the Route

Next, we find candidate optional stops (THCa/CBD stores) along the route corridor. We use the Google Places API – Text Search (New), leveraging its “search along route” capability. Google’s Places Text Search allows us to bias results to a given route polyline via the searchAlongRouteParameters in the request ￼. We will form a query for relevant businesses, for example a text query like "THCa OR CBD store" or simply "THCa CBD dispensary", depending on what yields best results for cannabis/hemp stores. In the Places API request, we include the searchAlongRouteParameters.polyline.encodedPolyline field set to the encoded route from step 1 ￼. This tells Google to return only places that lie near that route (within a certain corridor distance). The Places API will respond with a list of places matching the query and located near the specified route ￼. According to Google’s documentation, searching along a route returns places that require only minimal detours from the path ￼, so these candidates should be reasonably “on the way.”

From the Places API response, we will retrieve details of each candidate: name, address, location (lat/long), and perhaps a rating or other value indicator if we want to rank by quality. We likely need to filter this list down. For example, we might take the top 10 candidates by Google’s relevance/ranking. (Google’s along-route search inherently biases by route proximity and relevance, but we can additionally sort or filter if needed, e.g. eliminate duplicates or out-of-the-way points.) Each optional candidate will be initially treated as a potential stop with a default 15-minute service time (per requirements, optional stops have 15 min default dwell time).

3. Selecting the Best Optional Stops within Time Window

With the list of candidate THCa/CBD stores, the next step is to choose up to N of them (N = the user’s max optional stops) that can be fit into the trip without exceeding the end time. This is essentially an optimization problem – we want the highest-value optional stops that fit in the schedule. For the first functional version, we’ll implement a simple yet effective heuristic:
	•	Sort candidates by route order: We can determine where along the route each candidate lies (e.g. by projecting its location onto the route polyline or using relative distances from the start). This allows us to consider optional stops roughly in the order one would encounter them when traveling from the start to end. This prevents backtracking.
	•	Greedy inclusion: Starting from the beginning of the route, attempt to insert optional stops one by one, as long as adding each stop doesn’t break the time window. For each candidate (in sorted order), we simulate inserting it at the appropriate point in the route (between two existing stops). We use the Routes API to estimate the detour: specifically, we can call a quick route for the segment where the stop would be inserted. For example, if a candidate lies between must-stop A and the next stop B on the route, we compute travel time A -> candidate -> B. We compare this to the original travel time A -> B to get the extra travel cost of stopping by that candidate. Then we add the candidate’s 15 min service time to that. If the total trip duration with this stop remains ≤ (EndTime – StartTime) window, we include the stop. We then update the route (now including that stop) and continue to the next candidate. This greedy approach ensures we don’t exceed the schedule, though it may not always pick the mathematically optimal set, it should yield a reasonable selection given that the candidates are already near the route.
	•	Highest-value criterion: In this context, “highest-value” could be interpreted as the stops that maximize some benefit (perhaps those with the best ratings or those that are largest). In absence of a specific value metric from the user, we assume Google’s ranking combined with minimal detour serves as a proxy for value. Essentially, by following the sorted list of along-route search results, we are likely picking the most relevant stops first. We could refine this by preferring higher-rated places if needed.

This selection logic ensures all must-visit stops are included (they are mandatory), and then optional stops are filled in until we either reach the user’s maximum count or run out of available time. If the user set max optional stops to 0, we skip this entirely. If the time window is very tight (e.g. only enough time for the must stops), we might find that zero optional stops can fit – the tool should handle that gracefully (output just the must-stop itinerary and perhaps a note that no optional stops could be added due to time constraints).

Optional – Advanced Optimization: In future iterations, we can integrate Google’s Route Optimization API for a more sophisticated solution. The Route Optimization API (formerly “Cloud Fleet Routing”) can handle advanced routing scenarios with many stops, time windows, and even optional (skippable) stops. It can automatically optimize stop order and decide which optional stops to include or skip based on overall efficiency and constraints ￼. For example, we could model must-visit stores as required “shipments” and THCa/CBD stores as optional shipments with lower priority; the solver could then maximize the number of stops served within the driver’s available time (the vehicle’s time window) and skip those that don’t fit. This API supports setting time windows for arrivals at stops ￼, which aligns with our trip schedule. However, given the immediate need for a functional tool, we will first implement the simpler heuristic above. The simpler approach is quicker to implement and has fewer API dependencies; the Route Optimization API could be considered if we anticipate frequently needing to juggle many optional stops or complex constraints.

4. Final Itinerary Construction

After selecting the optional stops to include, we construct the final optimized list of stops in the order they will be visited. This sequence will be: Start → (some must and optional stops interwoven) → End. We will make one more call to the Routes API with this full ordered list to get final detailed directions and timing. This ensures we have accurate distances and travel times for each leg with all chosen stops in place. The request will include all stops as intermediate waypoints (marked as stops, not just pass-through, so that the API knows to treat them as break points for the route). The Routes API can handle up to 25 waypoints per request (including start/end), which is usually sufficient for our needs (if not, we might split it or use multiple calls). The response will give us each leg’s travel time and distance. Using the known service times for each stop, we then compute ETA for each stop: start at the trip Start Time, add travel time to first stop to get its arrival time, add service time to get departure, and so on until the final destination. This yields the timeline for the entire trip.

At this stage, we have all the data needed to produce the user-facing results: the ordered stops with their addresses, type (must/optional), and expected arrival times.

Generating the Itinerary Output

Once the calculations are done, the panel will display the results in several formats:
	•	Itinerary Table: The UI will show a table summarizing the trip. Each row represents a stop (or the start/end), with columns for: Order, Address, ETA (Estimated Arrival Time), and Type. The “Type” will indicate whether the stop was a “Must-visit” (required) or an “Optional” stop. For example, the first row might be “Start: [Start Address] – Departure at 8:00 AM” (we can label the origin as Start), then “1. [Store ABC Address] – ETA 9:15 AM – Must”, next “2. [THCa Store XYZ] – ETA 10:30 AM – Optional”, etc., until the final “End: [End Address] – ETA 4:45 PM”. This table gives the user a clear step-by-step plan. Styling-wise, we’ll emphasize the difference between must and optional (perhaps color-coding “Optional” or using icons). We will also ensure the table is easily readable – possibly using a fixed header and making it scrollable if too long. The goal is to let the user quickly scan the sequence of stops and timings.
	•	Google Maps Directions Links: To assist the user in navigation, we will provide Google Maps directions URLs that they can click on to open the route in Google Maps. However, Google Maps has a limit on how many waypoints can be included in a single directions link (often around 8 intermediate stops in the consumer Maps URL). To handle this, we will segment the route into multiple parts if necessary ￼. For example, if the trip has, say, 12 stops (including start and end), we might break it into two segments: one link for stops 1–10, and another link for stops 10–12. The first link would route from the Start through the first 8 waypoints to an intermediate stop, and the second link continues from that point to the final destination. This “chaining” ensures each link stays under the waypoint limit while collectively covering all stops ￼. In the UI, we can list these as “Direction Link Part 1, Part 2, …”. If the total number of stops is small (within the single-link limit), we’ll just show one link for the entire route. These links use Google Maps URLs (with the origin, destination, and waypoints parameters) so that the user (on their phone or laptop) can click and get the turn-by-turn directions in Google Maps immediately.
	•	Downloadable CSV: The panel will also offer a “Download CSV” button that produces a CSV file of the itinerary. The CSV will contain the stop order, address, ETA, type, and perhaps leg travel times or notes. The user can download this and open in Excel or share as needed. This is useful for record-keeping or if they want to tweak/present the plan. We will generate this CSV on the fly in the backend (or front-end) once the itinerary is finalized. In implementation, this could be done by formatting the itinerary data into CSV string format and triggering a download. For example, the backend could respond with a CSV file attachment, or the front-end could create a Blob URL. Given the tight timeline, a simple approach is fine. We ensure the CSV columns are well-labeled (e.g. “Stop #, Type, Address, ETA”).

Error Handling & Usability: Throughout the implementation, we will add checks and clear messages. For instance, if Google’s APIs return no candidates for THCa/CBD stores (it might happen if the route is very short or in an area without such stores), the tool will simply output “No optional stops found along the route” (and still show the must-stop route). If any API call fails (network or quota issues), the UI should handle it gracefully, showing an error message rather than crashing. We will also make sure that all API keys and calls are properly secured (the Places and Routes API calls can be made server-side via the Medusa backend to avoid exposing the API key publicly).

Implementation Notes
	•	Technology stack: The UI will be a React component in the Medusa Admin context. We can integrate it by creating an Admin UI extension or custom route (Medusa allows custom admin components via extension or by forking the admin panel if needed). The backend will use Node (the Medusa server) to call Google APIs via HTTP requests (likely using axios or fetch). We must enable the Google Places API, Routes API, and (optionally) the Route Optimization API in our Google Cloud project and secure the API key.
	•	Performance: The number of API calls will be moderate – one for the initial route, one for Places search, and one final route (plus a few small calls for each optional insertion check). This should be fine if done sequentially. We should be mindful of API quotas and perhaps use field masks to limit the data returned (e.g. we don’t need every place detail, just name and location). Given the urgent use-case, we prioritize correctness and clarity over extreme optimization.
	•	Robustness: We’ll test typical scenarios: round trip vs one-way, no optional stops vs some optional, edge cases like start and end very close (which per Google’s note might return no places ￼), etc. The UI should remain responsive during processing – we might add a loading spinner after “Plan Trip” is clicked until results are ready.
By following this design and implementation plan, we deliver a functional trip planner integrated into Medusa Admin that meets all the specified requirements. The sales team can use it immediately to chart their trip tomorrow, confident that the itinerary accounts for all required visits and includes any extra high-value stops that time permits.

You are a senior TypeScript engineer working inside an existing Medusa project. Implement a “Sales Trip Planner” tool as a Medusa Admin panel page and a Medusa admin API endpoint. This must be runnable today for a trip tomorrow. Keep scope minimal but complete.

Repo & constraints
	1.	First, inspect the repo structure and determine how Medusa Admin customization is set up:
	•	If the repo has src/admin/routes, implement the UI route there.
	•	If Admin is separate (e.g. /admin app), implement in the correct admin extension location.
	2.	Use TypeScript.
	3.	Do NOT scrape Google Maps UI. Use official Google APIs from the backend only.
	4.	Keep it “single user / single request” for now — no DB persistence required for v1.
	5.	Add clear README-style setup steps (env vars + enabling Google APIs).

Feature: Trip Planner UI (Admin panel)

Create a new Admin page named “Trip Planner” accessible from the sidebar.

UI requirements:
	•	Inputs:
	•	Start address (text input)
	•	End address (text input)
	•	Round trip checkbox:
	•	if checked: hide/disable End input and set end = start
	•	Trip date (date picker) + Start time + End time (time selectors)
	•	Max optional stops (dropdown selector: 0–10)
	•	Default optional stop service time (dropdown selector: 5, 10, 15, 20) default 15
	•	Must-visit stores section:
	•	“Add must stop” button
	•	Each must stop row has:
	•	Address input
	•	Service minutes dropdown selector default 30 (options: 10,15,20,30,45,60)
	•	Remove button
	•	Keywords section:
	•	default keywords: [“THCa”, “CBD store”, “hemp store”]
	•	allow editing (simple textarea one per line)
	•	Action:
	•	“Plan Trip” button calls backend endpoint.
	•	Output section:
	•	Table of ordered stops:
	•	Stop #, Type (Start/Must/Optional/End), Name (if available), Address, ETA, Depart time, Service minutes
	•	“Google Maps Links”:
	•	render segmented links (Segment 1, Segment 2, …), each opens a Google Maps directions URL
	•	“Download CSV” button:
	•	downloads itinerary CSV (stop #, type, name, address, eta, depart, serviceMinutes)

Backend: Admin API endpoint

Create a Medusa admin route:
	•	POST /admin/trip-planner/plan

Validate request with a schema (zod preferred). Request body:
type TripPlanRequest = {
  startAddress: string
  endAddress: string
  roundTrip: boolean
  dateISO: string           // "YYYY-MM-DD"
  startTime: string         // "HH:mm"
  endTime: string           // "HH:mm"
  mustStops: Array<{ address: string; serviceMinutes: number }>
  maxOptionalStops: number
  optionalServiceMinutes: number
  keywords: string[]        // ["THCa", ...]
  exportWaypointLimit: number // 3 or 9 (default 3 for “works on mobile”)
}
Response body:
type TripPlanResponse = {
  summary: {
    totalStops: number
    mustStops: number
    optionalStops: number
    totalDriveMinutesEstimate: number
    totalServiceMinutes: number
    fitsInWindow: boolean
    notes: string[]
  }
  stops: Array<{
    order: number
    type: "START" | "MUST" | "OPTIONAL" | "END"
    name?: string
    address: string
    lat?: number
    lng?: number
    etaISO?: string
    departISO?: string
    serviceMinutes: number
  }>
  googleMapsSegments: Array<{
    label: string
    url: string
    stopCount: number
  }>
  csv: string // CSV text (UI can also generate, but return here for simplicity)
}
Google APIs to use (server-side)

Use Google Maps Platform APIs:
	•	Routes API computeRoutes to get route duration/legs and encoded polyline.
	•	Places API (Text Search New) with “search along route polyline” to find stores between start and end:
	•	Use the route polyline from Routes as searchAlongRouteParameters.polyline.encodedPolyline
	•	Query each keyword (THCa, CBD store, hemp store) and merge/dedupe results.

Environment variables:
	•	GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY (used for Routes + Places)
Fail fast with a clear error if missing.

IMPORTANT implementation details:
	•	Use FieldMask headers for both Routes and Places requests to reduce payload.
	•	Keep API calls minimal and deterministic.

Planning algorithm (tomorrow-ready, not perfect)

Implement a simple, robust algorithm:
	1.	Normalize input:
	•	If roundTrip: endAddress = startAddress
	•	Stops list base: START + mustStops + END (serviceMinutes for START/END = 0)
	2.	Compute base route with Routes API using START → must stops → END (in user-entered order):
	•	Get total travel duration estimate in minutes.
	3.	Discover candidate optional places:
	•	Use Places Text Search “search along route” for each keyword with the base route polyline.
	•	Dedupe by place id and by near-identical lat/lng (within ~30m).
	•	Filter out any candidate whose address matches a must stop address (string contains is fine).
	•	Hard cap to top 40 candidates total for performance.
	4.	Select optional stops up to maxOptionalStops:
	•	Score candidates by “low detour proxy”:
	•	For v1, detour proxy = distance from candidate to nearest must-stop (haversine) OR just trust Places rank order
	•	Pick top N candidates.
	•	(Do NOT attempt heavy N^2 insertion optimization for v1.)
	5.	Final route:
	•	Compute final route with Routes API using START → (must + selected optional) → END.
	•	If total stops exceed what Routes supports, or Google returns errors, gracefully degrade:
	•	Drop optional stops until it works.
	6.	Time window fit:
	•	Compute totalTripMinutes = driveMinutes + sum(serviceMinutes)
	•	Determine fitsInWindow by comparing to (endTime - startTime)
	•	Compute ETA/departure timeline sequentially from startTime.
	7.	Exports:
	•	Google Maps segmented URLs:
	•	Build /maps/dir/?api=1&origin=...&destination=...&travelmode=driving&waypoints=...
	•	Segment using exportWaypointLimit (default 3) and also ensure URL length < 2000 chars:
	•	if exceeds, reduce segment size until it fits.
	•	CSV: create a CSV string from stops.

File layout (suggested)

Implement with clean separation:
	•	Admin UI:
	•	src/admin/routes/trip-planner/page.tsx
	•	src/admin/lib/sdk.ts (if not already present)
	•	Backend:
	•	src/api/admin/trip-planner/plan/route.ts
	•	src/services/trip-planner.ts (core logic)
	•	src/services/google/routes.ts (Routes API wrapper)
	•	src/services/google/places.ts (Places API wrapper)
	•	src/utils/maps-url.ts (Google Maps URL segment builder)
	•	src/utils/csv.ts (CSV builder)
	•	src/utils/time.ts (time window + ETA timeline helpers)

Definition of done
	•	I can run the Medusa backend + admin locally.
	•	In the admin sidebar I can open “Trip Planner”.
	•	I can input:
	•	start address, end address (or round trip),
	•	must stop list with 30 min service times,
	•	trip time window,
	•	max optional stops,
	•	keywords,
	•	Click “Plan Trip”
	•	I see:
	•	ordered itinerary with ETAs,
	•	at least one Google Maps segment link that opens correctly,
	•	CSV download that matches the itinerary.
	•	If Google API key is missing, I get a clear error in UI.
	•	If optional stop discovery fails, it still plans the trip with must-stops only and returns a note.

Deliverables in your response
	•	Show the file tree changes.
	•	Provide full code for each new/modified file.
	•	Provide setup instructions:
	•	env vars
	•	which Google APIs must be enabled
	•	how to run
	•	Include a short “Known limitations” section (v1 heuristic, waypoint/export limits, etc.).

Now implement it.

If the agent returns something half-done, these follow-ups usually fix it fast:
	1.	“Refactor the Google API calls into wrappers with FieldMask headers and strict error handling.”
	2.	“Make the Google Maps URL exporter segment by waypoint limit AND URL length < 2000 chars; add tests.”
	3.	“Improve optional-stop selection: compute a cheap detour proxy using haversine distance to the base route polyline (decode polyline), still without heavy API calls.”
	4.	“Add a UI toggle: preserve must-stop order vs allow reorder (use Routes optimizeWaypointOrder).”

**Cursor**

```typescript
type ComputeRouteInput = {
  originAddress: string
  destinationAddress: string
  intermediateAddresses?: string[]
  departureTimeISO?: string
}

type RouteResult = {
  encodedPolyline: string | null
  legDurationsMinutes: number[]
  totalDriveMinutes: number
}

const ROUTES_URL = "https://routes.googleapis.com/directions/v2:computeRoutes"

const ROUTES_FIELD_MASK =
  "routes.duration,routes.legs.duration,routes.polyline.encodedPolyline"

const toMinutes = (duration: unknown) => {
  if (!duration) {
    return 0
  }

  if (typeof duration === "string") {
    const seconds = Number(duration.replace("s", ""))
    return Number.isFinite(seconds) ? Math.ceil(seconds / 60) : 0
  }

  if (
    typeof duration === "object" &&
    duration !== null &&
    "seconds" in duration
  ) {
    const seconds = Number((duration as { seconds?: string | number }).seconds)
    if (!Number.isFinite(seconds)) {
      return 0
    }
    return Math.ceil(seconds / 60)
  }

  return 0
}

export async function computeRoute(
  input: ComputeRouteInput,
  apiKey: string
): Promise<RouteResult> {
  const { originAddress, destinationAddress, intermediateAddresses, departureTimeISO } =
    input

  const body = {
    origin: { address: originAddress },
    destination: { address: destinationAddress },
    intermediates: (intermediateAddresses || []).map((address) => ({
      address,
    })),
    travelMode: "DRIVE",
    routingPreference: "TRAFFIC_AWARE",
    polylineQuality: "OVERVIEW",
    polylineEncoding: "ENCODED_POLYLINE",
    ...(departureTimeISO ? { departureTime: departureTimeISO } : {}),
  }

  const response = await fetch(ROUTES_URL, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "X-Goog-Api-Key": apiKey,
      "X-Goog-FieldMask": ROUTES_FIELD_MASK,
    },
    body: JSON.stringify(body),
  })

  if (!response.ok) {
    const errorText = await response.text()
    throw new Error(
      `Routes API error (${response.status}): ${errorText || response.statusText}`
    )
  }

  const data = (await response.json()) as {
    routes?: Array<{
      duration?: string | { seconds?: string | number }
      legs?: Array<{ duration?: string | { seconds?: string | number } }>
      polyline?: { encodedPolyline?: string }
    }>
  }

  const route = data.routes?.[0]

  if (!route) {
    throw new Error("Routes API error: no routes returned.")
  }

  const legDurationsMinutes = (route.legs || []).map((leg) =>
    toMinutes(leg.duration)
  )

  const totalDriveMinutes =
    legDurationsMinutes.reduce((sum, minutes) => sum + minutes, 0) ||
    toMinutes(route.duration)

  return {
    encodedPolyline: route.polyline?.encodedPolyline || null,
    legDurationsMinutes,
    totalDriveMinutes,
  }
}

```

```typescript
type SearchPlacesInput = {
  query: string
  encodedPolyline: string
  apiKey: string
  maxResults?: number
}

export type PlaceCandidate = {
  id: string
  name: string
  address: string
  lat: number
  lng: number
}

const PLACES_URL = "https://places.googleapis.com/v1/places:searchText"

const PLACES_FIELD_MASK =
  "places.id,places.displayName,places.formattedAddress,places.location"

export async function searchPlacesAlongRoute({
  query,
  encodedPolyline,
  apiKey,
  maxResults = 20,
}: SearchPlacesInput): Promise<PlaceCandidate[]> {
  const body = {
    textQuery: query,
    maxResultCount: maxResults,
    searchAlongRouteParameters: {
      polyline: {
        encodedPolyline,
      },
    },
  }

  const response = await fetch(PLACES_URL, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "X-Goog-Api-Key": apiKey,
      "X-Goog-FieldMask": PLACES_FIELD_MASK,
    },
    body: JSON.stringify(body),
  })

  if (!response.ok) {
    const errorText = await response.text()
    throw new Error(
      `Places API error (${response.status}): ${errorText || response.statusText}`
    )
  }

  const data = (await response.json()) as {
    places?: Array<{
      id?: string
      displayName?: { text?: string }
      formattedAddress?: string
      location?: { latitude?: number; longitude?: number }
    }>
  }

  const places = data.places || []

  return places
    .map((place) => {
      const id = place.id || ""
      const name = place.displayName?.text || ""
      const address = place.formattedAddress || ""
      const lat = place.location?.latitude
      const lng = place.location?.longitude

      if (!id || !address || typeof lat !== "number" || typeof lng !== "number") {
        return null
      }

      return {
        id,
        name,
        address,
        lat,
        lng,
      }
    })
    .filter((place): place is PlaceCandidate => Boolean(place))
}

```

```typescript
const pad = (value: number) => value.toString().padStart(2, "0")

export const parseLocalDateTime = (dateISO: string, time: string) => {
  const [year, month, day] = dateISO.split("-").map(Number)
  const [hours, minutes] = time.split(":").map(Number)
  return new Date(year, (month || 1) - 1, day || 1, hours || 0, minutes || 0, 0, 0)
}

export const diffMinutes = (start: Date, end: Date) => {
  return Math.max(0, Math.round((end.getTime() - start.getTime()) / 60000))
}

export const addMinutes = (date: Date, minutes: number) => {
  return new Date(date.getTime() + minutes * 60000)
}

export const formatLocalISOString = (date: Date) => {
  const year = date.getFullYear()
  const month = pad(date.getMonth() + 1)
  const day = pad(date.getDate())
  const hours = pad(date.getHours())
  const minutes = pad(date.getMinutes())
  const seconds = pad(date.getSeconds())
  const offsetMinutes = -date.getTimezoneOffset()
  const sign = offsetMinutes >= 0 ? "+" : "-"
  const absOffset = Math.abs(offsetMinutes)
  const offsetHours = pad(Math.floor(absOffset / 60))
  const offsetMins = pad(absOffset % 60)

  return `${year}-${month}-${day}T${hours}:${minutes}:${seconds}${sign}${offsetHours}:${offsetMins}`
}

```

```typescript
type CsvRow = Array<string | number | undefined | null>

const escapeValue = (value: string) => {
  const needsQuotes = /[",\n]/.test(value)
  const escaped = value.replace(/"/g, '""')
  return needsQuotes ? `"${escaped}"` : escaped
}

export const buildCsv = (headers: string[], rows: CsvRow[]) => {
  const headerLine = headers.map(escapeValue).join(",")
  const rowLines = rows.map((row) =>
    row
      .map((value) =>
        escapeValue(value === undefined || value === null ? "" : String(value))
      )
      .join(",")
  )

  return [headerLine, ...rowLines].join("\n")
}

```

```typescript
type MapStop = {
  address: string
}

export type MapsSegment = {
  label: string
  url: string
  stopCount: number
}

const MAX_URL_LENGTH = 2000

const encodeAddress = (address: string) => encodeURIComponent(address)

const buildUrl = (origin: string, destination: string, waypoints: string[]) => {
  const params = new URLSearchParams({
    api: "1",
    origin: encodeAddress(origin),
    destination: encodeAddress(destination),
    travelmode: "driving",
  })

  if (waypoints.length) {
    params.set("waypoints", waypoints.map(encodeAddress).join("|"))
  }

  return `https://www.google.com/maps/dir/?${params.toString()}`
}

export const buildMapsSegments = (
  stops: MapStop[],
  waypointLimit: number
): MapsSegment[] => {
  if (stops.length < 2) {
    return []
  }

  const segments: MapsSegment[] = []
  let segmentIndex = 0
  let cursor = 0

  while (cursor < stops.length - 1) {
    let endIndex = Math.min(cursor + waypointLimit + 1, stops.length - 1)
    let url = ""

    while (endIndex > cursor) {
      const waypoints = stops
        .slice(cursor + 1, endIndex)
        .map((stop) => stop.address)

      url = buildUrl(
        stops[cursor].address,
        stops[endIndex].address,
        waypoints
      )

      if (url.length <= MAX_URL_LENGTH) {
        break
      }

      endIndex -= 1
    }

    segments.push({
      label: `Segment ${segmentIndex + 1}`,
      url,
      stopCount: endIndex - cursor + 1,
    })

    segmentIndex += 1
    cursor = endIndex
  }

  return segments
}

```

```typescript
import { computeRoute } from "./google/routes"
import { PlaceCandidate, searchPlacesAlongRoute } from "./google/places"
import { addMinutes, diffMinutes, formatLocalISOString, parseLocalDateTime } from "../utils/time"
import { buildMapsSegments, MapsSegment } from "../utils/maps-url"
import { buildCsv } from "../utils/csv"

export type TripPlanRequest = {
  startAddress: string
  endAddress: string
  roundTrip: boolean
  dateISO: string
  startTime: string
  endTime: string
  mustStops: Array<{ address: string; serviceMinutes: number }>
  maxOptionalStops: number
  optionalServiceMinutes: number
  keywords: string[]
  exportWaypointLimit: number
}

export type TripPlanResponse = {
  summary: {
    totalStops: number
    mustStops: number
    optionalStops: number
    totalDriveMinutesEstimate: number
    totalServiceMinutes: number
    fitsInWindow: boolean
    notes: string[]
  }
  stops: Array<{
    order: number
    type: "START" | "MUST" | "OPTIONAL" | "END"
    name?: string
    address: string
    lat?: number
    lng?: number
    etaISO?: string
    departISO?: string
    serviceMinutes: number
  }>
  googleMapsSegments: MapsSegment[]
  csv: string
}

type PlannedStop = {
  type: "START" | "MUST" | "OPTIONAL" | "END"
  name?: string
  address: string
  lat?: number
  lng?: number
  serviceMinutes: number
}

const MAX_OPTIONAL_CANDIDATES = 40

const haversineMeters = (
  lat1: number,
  lng1: number,
  lat2: number,
  lng2: number
) => {
  const toRad = (value: number) => (value * Math.PI) / 180
  const earthRadius = 6371000
  const dLat = toRad(lat2 - lat1)
  const dLng = toRad(lng2 - lng1)
  const a =
    Math.sin(dLat / 2) ** 2 +
    Math.cos(toRad(lat1)) *
      Math.cos(toRad(lat2)) *
      Math.sin(dLng / 2) ** 2
  const c = 2 * Math.atan2(Math.sqrt(a), Math.sqrt(1 - a))
  return earthRadius * c
}

const dedupeCandidates = (candidates: PlaceCandidate[]) => {
  const byId = new Map<string, PlaceCandidate>()
  const deduped: PlaceCandidate[] = []

  for (const candidate of candidates) {
    if (byId.has(candidate.id)) {
      continue
    }

    const nearMatch = deduped.find(
      (existing) =>
        haversineMeters(existing.lat, existing.lng, candidate.lat, candidate.lng) <
        30
    )

    if (nearMatch) {
      continue
    }

    byId.set(candidate.id, candidate)
    deduped.push(candidate)
  }

  return deduped
}

const normalizeAddress = (address: string) =>
  address.trim().toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, " ")

const buildTimeline = (
  stops: PlannedStop[],
  legDurationsMinutes: number[],
  startDateTime: Date
) => {
  const timeline: Array<{
    etaISO: string
    departISO: string
  }> = []

  let cursor = startDateTime
  stops.forEach((stop, index) => {
    if (index === 0) {
      const eta = formatLocalISOString(cursor)
      const depart = formatLocalISOString(addMinutes(cursor, stop.serviceMinutes))
      timeline.push({ etaISO: eta, departISO: depart })
      cursor = addMinutes(cursor, stop.serviceMinutes)
      return
    }

    const travelMinutes = legDurationsMinutes[index - 1] || 0
    cursor = addMinutes(cursor, travelMinutes)
    const eta = formatLocalISOString(cursor)
    cursor = addMinutes(cursor, stop.serviceMinutes)
    const depart = formatLocalISOString(cursor)
    timeline.push({ etaISO: eta, departISO: depart })
  })

  return timeline
}

export const planTrip = async (
  request: TripPlanRequest
): Promise<TripPlanResponse> => {
  const apiKey = process.env.GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY
  if (!apiKey) {
    throw new Error("Missing GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY. Configure it in the environment.")
  }

  const notes: string[] = []
  const startAddress = request.startAddress.trim()
  const endAddress = request.roundTrip
    ? startAddress
    : request.endAddress.trim()

  const mustStops = request.mustStops
    .map((stop) => ({
      address: stop.address.trim(),
      serviceMinutes: stop.serviceMinutes,
    }))
    .filter((stop) => stop.address.length > 0)

  const departureTimeISO = formatLocalISOString(
    parseLocalDateTime(request.dateISO, request.startTime)
  )

  const baseRoute = await computeRoute(
    {
      originAddress: startAddress,
      destinationAddress: endAddress,
      intermediateAddresses: mustStops.map((stop) => stop.address),
      departureTimeISO,
    },
    apiKey
  )

  let optionalCandidates: PlaceCandidate[] = []

  if (request.maxOptionalStops > 0 && baseRoute.encodedPolyline) {
    try {
      const keywordResults = await Promise.all(
        request.keywords.map((keyword) =>
          searchPlacesAlongRoute({
            query: keyword,
            encodedPolyline: baseRoute.encodedPolyline!,
            apiKey,
            maxResults: 20,
          })
        )
      )

      optionalCandidates = dedupeCandidates(keywordResults.flat())
    } catch (error) {
      notes.push(
        "Optional stop discovery failed. Planned route will include must-stop locations only."
      )
    }
  }

  if (optionalCandidates.length > MAX_OPTIONAL_CANDIDATES) {
    optionalCandidates = optionalCandidates.slice(0, MAX_OPTIONAL_CANDIDATES)
  }

  const normalizedMustAddresses = mustStops.map((stop) =>
    normalizeAddress(stop.address)
  )

  optionalCandidates = optionalCandidates.filter((candidate) => {
    const normalized = normalizeAddress(candidate.address)
    return !normalizedMustAddresses.some((mustAddress) =>
      normalized.includes(mustAddress)
    )
  })

  const selectedOptional = optionalCandidates
    .slice(0, request.maxOptionalStops)
    .map((candidate) => ({
      type: "OPTIONAL" as const,
      name: candidate.name,
      address: candidate.address,
      lat: candidate.lat,
      lng: candidate.lng,
      serviceMinutes: request.optionalServiceMinutes,
    }))

  let finalStops: PlannedStop[] = [
    {
      type: "START",
      address: startAddress,
      serviceMinutes: 0,
    },
    ...mustStops.map((stop) => ({
      type: "MUST" as const,
      address: stop.address,
      serviceMinutes: stop.serviceMinutes,
    })),
    ...selectedOptional,
    {
      type: "END",
      address: endAddress,
      serviceMinutes: 0,
    },
  ]

  let legDurationsMinutes: number[] = []
  let totalDriveMinutes = 0

  let optionalToTry = selectedOptional.length

  while (true) {
    try {
      const intermediates = finalStops.slice(1, -1).map((stop) => stop.address)
      const route = await computeRoute(
        {
          originAddress: startAddress,
          destinationAddress: endAddress,
          intermediateAddresses: intermediates,
          departureTimeISO,
        },
        apiKey
      )
      legDurationsMinutes = route.legDurationsMinutes
      totalDriveMinutes = route.totalDriveMinutes
      break
    } catch (error) {
      if (optionalToTry > 0) {
        optionalToTry -= 1
        notes.push(
          "Dropped an optional stop to satisfy routing limits. Reduce optional stops if you want to keep all candidates."
        )
        finalStops = [
          finalStops[0],
          ...finalStops.slice(1, 1 + mustStops.length),
          ...selectedOptional.slice(0, optionalToTry),
          finalStops[finalStops.length - 1],
        ]
        continue
      }
      throw error
    }
  }

  const startDateTime = parseLocalDateTime(request.dateISO, request.startTime)
  const endDateTime = parseLocalDateTime(request.dateISO, request.endTime)
  const windowMinutes = diffMinutes(startDateTime, endDateTime)

  const totalServiceMinutes = finalStops.reduce(
    (sum, stop) => sum + stop.serviceMinutes,
    0
  )

  const totalTripMinutes = totalDriveMinutes + totalServiceMinutes
  const fitsInWindow = totalTripMinutes <= windowMinutes

  const timeline = buildTimeline(finalStops, legDurationsMinutes, startDateTime)

  const stopsWithTiming = finalStops.map((stop, index) => ({
    order: index + 1,
    type: stop.type,
    name: stop.name,
    address: stop.address,
    lat: stop.lat,
    lng: stop.lng,
    etaISO: timeline[index]?.etaISO,
    departISO: timeline[index]?.departISO,
    serviceMinutes: stop.serviceMinutes,
  }))

  const googleMapsSegments = buildMapsSegments(
    finalStops.map((stop) => ({ address: stop.address })),
    request.exportWaypointLimit || 3
  )

  const csv = buildCsv(
    ["Stop #", "Type", "Name", "Address", "ETA", "Depart", "Service Minutes"],
    stopsWithTiming.map((stop) => [
      stop.order,
      stop.type,
      stop.name || "",
      stop.address,
      stop.etaISO || "",
      stop.departISO || "",
      stop.serviceMinutes,
    ])
  )

  return {
    summary: {
      totalStops: finalStops.length,
      mustStops: mustStops.length,
      optionalStops: finalStops.filter((stop) => stop.type === "OPTIONAL").length,
      totalDriveMinutesEstimate: totalDriveMinutes,
      totalServiceMinutes,
      fitsInWindow,
      notes,
    },
    stops: stopsWithTiming,
    googleMapsSegments,
    csv,
  }
}

```

```typescript
import { MedusaRequest, MedusaResponse } from "@medusajs/framework/http"
import { z } from "zod"
import { planTrip } from "../../../../services/trip-planner"

const timeRegex = /^\d{2}:\d{2}$/

const TripPlanSchema = z.object({
  startAddress: z.string().trim().min(1),
  endAddress: z.string().trim().min(1),
  roundTrip: z.boolean(),
  dateISO: z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/),
  startTime: z.string().regex(timeRegex),
  endTime: z.string().regex(timeRegex),
  mustStops: z
    .array(
      z.object({
        address: z.string().trim().min(1),
        serviceMinutes: z.number().int().min(0),
      })
    )
    .default([]),
  maxOptionalStops: z.number().int().min(0).max(10),
  optionalServiceMinutes: z.number().int().min(5).max(60),
  keywords: z.array(z.string().trim().min(1)).default([]),
  exportWaypointLimit: z.number().int().min(1).max(9).default(3),
})

export async function POST(req: MedusaRequest, res: MedusaResponse) {
  const parsed = TripPlanSchema.safeParse(req.body)

  if (!parsed.success) {
    return res.status(400).json({
      message: "Invalid request body for trip planner.",
      errors: parsed.error.flatten(),
    })
  }

  try {
    const response = await planTrip(parsed.data)
    return res.status(200).json(response)
  } catch (error) {
    const message =
      error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Trip planning failed."
    return res.status(500).json({ message })
  }
}
```

```typescript
export type ApiError = {
  message: string
}

export const postAdmin = async <TResponse>(
  path: string,
  body: unknown
): Promise<TResponse> => {
  const response = await fetch(path, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
    },
    credentials: "include",
    body: JSON.stringify(body),
  })

  if (!response.ok) {
    let message = `Request failed (${response.status})`
    try {
      const errorBody = (await response.json()) as ApiError
      if (errorBody?.message) {
        message = errorBody.message
      }
    } catch (error) {
      const text = await response.text()
      if (text) {
        message = text
      }
    }

    throw new Error(message)
  }

  return (await response.json()) as TResponse
}

```

```typescriptreact
import { defineRouteConfig } from "@medusajs/admin-sdk"
import { MapPin } from "@medusajs/icons"
import {
  Button,
  Container,
  Heading,
  Input,
  Label,
  Select,
  Table,
  Text,
  Textarea,
  Checkbox,
} from "@medusajs/ui"
import { useMemo, useState } from "react"
import { postAdmin } from "../../lib/sdk"

type TripStop = {
  order: number
  type: "START" | "MUST" | "OPTIONAL" | "END"
  name?: string
  address: string
  etaISO?: string
  departISO?: string
  serviceMinutes: number
}

type TripPlanResponse = {
  summary: {
    totalStops: number
    mustStops: number
    optionalStops: number
    totalDriveMinutesEstimate: number
    totalServiceMinutes: number
    fitsInWindow: boolean
    notes: string[]
  }
  stops: TripStop[]
  googleMapsSegments: Array<{
    label: string
    url: string
    stopCount: number
  }>
  csv: string
}

const buildTodayISO = () => {
  const now = new Date()
  const year = now.getFullYear()
  const month = String(now.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, "0")
  const day = String(now.getDate()).padStart(2, "0")
  return `${year}-${month}-${day}`
}

const DEFAULT_KEYWORDS = ["THCa", "CBD store", "hemp store"].join("\n")

const TripPlannerPage = () => {
  const [startAddress, setStartAddress] = useState("")
  const [endAddress, setEndAddress] = useState("")
  const [roundTrip, setRoundTrip] = useState(false)
  const [dateISO, setDateISO] = useState(buildTodayISO())
  const [startTime, setStartTime] = useState("08:00")
  const [endTime, setEndTime] = useState("17:00")
  const [maxOptionalStops, setMaxOptionalStops] = useState("2")
  const [optionalServiceMinutes, setOptionalServiceMinutes] = useState("15")
  const [mustStops, setMustStops] = useState<
    Array<{ address: string; serviceMinutes: string }>
  >([{ address: "", serviceMinutes: "30" }])
  const [keywordsText, setKeywordsText] = useState(DEFAULT_KEYWORDS)
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false)
  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)
  const [result, setResult] = useState<TripPlanResponse | null>(null)

  const keywords = useMemo(
    () =>
      keywordsText
        .split("\n")
        .map((line) => line.trim())
        .filter(Boolean),
    [keywordsText]
  )

  const handleRoundTrip = (checked: boolean) => {
    setRoundTrip(checked)
    if (checked) {
      setEndAddress(startAddress)
    }
  }

  const handleStartAddressChange = (value: string) => {
    setStartAddress(value)
    if (roundTrip) {
      setEndAddress(value)
    }
  }

  const updateMustStop = (
    index: number,
    changes: Partial<{ address: string; serviceMinutes: string }>
  ) => {
    setMustStops((prev) =>
      prev.map((stop, idx) =>
        idx === index ? { ...stop, ...changes } : stop
      )
    )
  }

  const addMustStop = () => {
    setMustStops((prev) => [...prev, { address: "", serviceMinutes: "30" }])
  }

  const removeMustStop = (index: number) => {
    setMustStops((prev) => prev.filter((_, idx) => idx !== index))
  }

  const handleSubmit = async () => {
    setError(null)
    setResult(null)

    if (!startAddress.trim()) {
      setError("Start address is required.")
      return
    }

    if (!roundTrip && !endAddress.trim()) {
      setError("End address is required.")
      return
    }

    if (!dateISO || !startTime || !endTime) {
      setError("Trip date, start time, and end time are required.")
      return
    }

    setIsLoading(true)
    try {
      const response = await postAdmin<TripPlanResponse>(
        "/admin/trip-planner/plan",
        {
          startAddress: startAddress.trim(),
          endAddress: endAddress.trim() || startAddress.trim(),
          roundTrip,
          dateISO,
          startTime,
          endTime,
          mustStops: mustStops
            .map((stop) => ({
              address: stop.address.trim(),
              serviceMinutes: Number(stop.serviceMinutes),
            }))
            .filter((stop) => stop.address.length > 0),
          maxOptionalStops: Number(maxOptionalStops),
          optionalServiceMinutes: Number(optionalServiceMinutes),
          keywords,
          exportWaypointLimit: 3,
        }
      )
      setResult(response)
    } catch (submitError) {
      setError(
        submitError instanceof Error
          ? submitError.message
          : "Failed to plan trip."
      )
    } finally {
      setIsLoading(false)
    }
  }

  const downloadCsv = () => {
    if (!result?.csv) {
      return
    }
    const blob = new Blob([result.csv], { type: "text/csv;charset=utf-8;" })
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
    const link = document.createElement("a")
    link.href = url
    link.download = `trip-itinerary-${dateISO}.csv`
    document.body.appendChild(link)
    link.click()
    link.remove()
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url)
  }

  return (
    <Container className="flex flex-col gap-6">
      <div>
        <Heading level="h1">Trip Planner</Heading>
        <Text size="small" className="text-ui-fg-subtle">
          Plan a sales trip with required stops and optional THCa/CBD store visits.
        </Text>
      </div>

      <Container className="flex flex-col gap-4">
        <Heading level="h2">Trip Details</Heading>
        <div className="grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 md:grid-cols-2">
          <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
            <Label>Start address</Label>
            <Input
              value={startAddress}
              onChange={(event) => handleStartAddressChange(event.target.value)}
              placeholder="Enter start address"
            />
          </div>
          <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
            <Label>Round trip</Label>
            <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
              <Checkbox
                checked={roundTrip}
                onCheckedChange={(checked) =>
                  handleRoundTrip(checked === true)
                }
              />
              <Text size="small">End where you started</Text>
            </div>
          </div>
          {!roundTrip && (
            <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
              <Label>End address</Label>
              <Input
                value={endAddress}
                onChange={(event) => setEndAddress(event.target.value)}
                placeholder="Enter end address"
              />
            </div>
          )}
        </div>

        <div className="grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 md:grid-cols-3">
          <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
            <Label>Trip date</Label>
            <Input
              type="date"
              value={dateISO}
              onChange={(event) => setDateISO(event.target.value)}
            />
          </div>
          <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
            <Label>Start time</Label>
            <Input
              type="time"
              value={startTime}
              onChange={(event) => setStartTime(event.target.value)}
            />
          </div>
          <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
            <Label>End time</Label>
            <Input
              type="time"
              value={endTime}
              onChange={(event) => setEndTime(event.target.value)}
            />
          </div>
        </div>

        <div className="grid grid-cols-1 gap-4 md:grid-cols-2">
          <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
            <Label>Max optional stops</Label>
            <Select
              value={maxOptionalStops}
              onValueChange={(value) => setMaxOptionalStops(value)}
            >
              <Select.Trigger>
                <Select.Value placeholder="Select max stops" />
              </Select.Trigger>
              <Select.Content>
                {Array.from({ length: 11 }, (_, index) => (
                  <Select.Item key={index} value={`${index}`}>
                    {index}
                  </Select.Item>
                ))}
              </Select.Content>
            </Select>
          </div>
          <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
            <Label>Optional stop service minutes</Label>
            <Select
              value={optionalServiceMinutes}
              onValueChange={(value) => setOptionalServiceMinutes(value)}
            >
              <Select.Trigger>
                <Select.Value placeholder="Select minutes" />
              </Select.Trigger>
              <Select.Content>
                {[5, 10, 15, 20].map((minutes) => (
                  <Select.Item key={minutes} value={`${minutes}`}>
                    {minutes}
                  </Select.Item>
                ))}
              </Select.Content>
            </Select>
          </div>
        </div>
      </Container>

      <Container className="flex flex-col gap-4">
        <Heading level="h2">Must-Visit Stores</Heading>
        <div className="flex flex-col gap-3">
          {mustStops.map((stop, index) => (
            <div
              key={`must-stop-${index}`}
              className="grid grid-cols-1 gap-3 md:grid-cols-[1fr_160px_100px]"
            >
              <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
                <Label>Address</Label>
                <Input
                  value={stop.address}
                  onChange={(event) =>
                    updateMustStop(index, { address: event.target.value })
                  }
                  placeholder="Enter must-visit address"
                />
              </div>
              <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
                <Label>Service minutes</Label>
                <Select
                  value={stop.serviceMinutes}
                  onValueChange={(value) =>
                    updateMustStop(index, { serviceMinutes: value })
                  }
                >
                  <Select.Trigger>
                    <Select.Value placeholder="Minutes" />
                  </Select.Trigger>
                  <Select.Content>
                    {[10, 15, 20, 30, 45, 60].map((minutes) => (
                      <Select.Item key={minutes} value={`${minutes}`}>
                        {minutes}
                      </Select.Item>
                    ))}
                  </Select.Content>
                </Select>
              </div>
              <div className="flex items-end">
                <Button
                  variant="secondary"
                  onClick={() => removeMustStop(index)}
                  disabled={mustStops.length === 1}
                >
                  Remove
                </Button>
              </div>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
        <div>
          <Button variant="secondary" onClick={addMustStop}>
            Add must stop
          </Button>
        </div>
      </Container>

      <Container className="flex flex-col gap-4">
        <Heading level="h2">Keywords</Heading>
        <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
          <Label>One per line</Label>
          <Textarea
            value={keywordsText}
            onChange={(event) => setKeywordsText(event.target.value)}
            rows={4}
          />
          <Text size="small" className="text-ui-fg-subtle">
            We use these keywords to find optional THCa/CBD stores along the route.
          </Text>
        </div>
      </Container>

      <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
        {error && <Text className="text-ui-fg-error">{error}</Text>}
        <Button onClick={handleSubmit} isLoading={isLoading}>
          Plan Trip
        </Button>
      </div>

      {result && (
        <Container className="flex flex-col gap-6">
          <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
            <Heading level="h2">Itinerary Summary</Heading>
            <Text size="small">
              {result.summary.totalStops} total stops ·{" "}
              {result.summary.mustStops} must ·{" "}
              {result.summary.optionalStops} optional ·{" "}
              {result.summary.totalDriveMinutesEstimate} drive minutes ·{" "}
              {result.summary.totalServiceMinutes} service minutes
            </Text>
            <Text size="small">
              Fits in window: {result.summary.fitsInWindow ? "Yes" : "No"}
            </Text>
            {result.summary.notes.length > 0 && (
              <div className="flex flex-col gap-1">
                {result.summary.notes.map((note, index) => (
                  <Text key={`note-${index}`} size="small">
                    {note}
                  </Text>
                ))}
              </div>
            )}
          </div>

          <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
            <Heading level="h2">Stops</Heading>
            <Table>
              <Table.Header>
                <Table.Row>
                  <Table.HeaderCell>Stop #</Table.HeaderCell>
                  <Table.HeaderCell>Type</Table.HeaderCell>
                  <Table.HeaderCell>Name</Table.HeaderCell>
                  <Table.HeaderCell>Address</Table.HeaderCell>
                  <Table.HeaderCell>ETA</Table.HeaderCell>
                  <Table.HeaderCell>Depart</Table.HeaderCell>
                  <Table.HeaderCell>Service</Table.HeaderCell>
                </Table.Row>
              </Table.Header>
              <Table.Body>
                {result.stops.map((stop) => (
                  <Table.Row key={`stop-${stop.order}`}>
                    <Table.Cell>{stop.order}</Table.Cell>
                    <Table.Cell>{stop.type}</Table.Cell>
                    <Table.Cell>{stop.name || "-"}</Table.Cell>
                    <Table.Cell>{stop.address}</Table.Cell>
                    <Table.Cell>{stop.etaISO || "-"}</Table.Cell>
                    <Table.Cell>{stop.departISO || "-"}</Table.Cell>
                    <Table.Cell>{stop.serviceMinutes} min</Table.Cell>
                  </Table.Row>
                ))}
              </Table.Body>
            </Table>
          </div>

          <div className="flex flex-col gap-2">
            <Heading level="h2">Google Maps Links</Heading>
            <div className="flex flex-col gap-1">
              {result.googleMapsSegments.map((segment) => (
                <a
                  key={segment.label}
                  href={segment.url}
                  target="_blank"
                  rel="noreferrer"
                  className="text-ui-fg-interactive"
                >
                  {segment.label} ({segment.stopCount} stops)
                </a>
              ))}
            </div>
          </div>

          <div>
            <Button variant="secondary" onClick={downloadCsv}>
              Download CSV
            </Button>
          </div>
        </Container>
      )}
    </Container>
  )
}

export const config = defineRouteConfig({
  label: "Trip Planner",
  icon: MapPin,
})

export default TripPlannerPage

```

```markdown
## Compatibility

This starter is compatible with versions >= 2 of `@medusajs/medusa`. 

## Getting Started

Visit the [Quickstart Guide](https://docs.medusajs.com/learn/installation) to set up a server.

Visit the [Docs](https://docs.medusajs.com/learn/installation#get-started) to learn more about our system requirements.

## Trip Planner (Admin)

This project includes a Medusa Admin page for planning sales trips with required and optional stops.

### Setup

1. Enable Google Maps Platform APIs:
   - Routes API
   - Places API (Text Search)
2. Add the API key to your environment:
   - `GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY=your_key_here`
3. Start the backend and admin:
   - `yarn dev`

### Known limitations (v1)

- Optional stop selection uses a simple rank-based heuristic (no full route optimization).
- Optional stops are appended after must stops to keep routing deterministic.
- Google Maps export links are segmented by waypoint limit and URL length, so long routes may create multiple segments.

## What is Medusa

Medusa is a set of commerce modules and tools that allow you to build rich, reliable, and performant commerce applications without reinventing core commerce logic. The modules can be customized and used to build advanced ecommerce stores, marketplaces, or any product that needs foundational commerce primitives. All modules are open-source and freely available on npm.

Learn more about [Medusa’s architecture](https://docs.medusajs.com/learn/introduction/architecture) and [commerce modules](https://docs.medusajs.com/learn/fundamentals/modules/commerce-modules) in the Docs.

## Community & Contributions

The community and core team are available in [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/medusajs/medusa/discussions), where you can ask for support, discuss roadmap, and share ideas.

Join our [Discord server](https://discord.com/invite/medusajs) to meet other community members.

## Other channels

- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/medusajs/medusa/issues)
- [Twitter](https://twitter.com/medusajs)
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/medusajs)
- [Medusa Blog](https://medusajs.com/blog/)

```

```typescript
type MapStop = {
  address: string
}

export type MapsSegment = {
  label: string
  url: string
  stopCount: number
}

const MAX_URL_LENGTH = 2000

const buildUrl = (origin: string, destination: string, waypoints: string[]) => {
  const params = new URLSearchParams({
    api: "1",
    origin,
    destination,
    travelmode: "driving",
  })

  if (waypoints.length) {
    params.set("waypoints", waypoints.join("|"))
  }

  return `https://www.google.com/maps/dir/?${params.toString()}`
}

export const buildMapsSegments = (
  stops: MapStop[],
  waypointLimit: number
): MapsSegment[] => {
  if (stops.length < 2) {
    return []
  }

  const segments: MapsSegment[] = []
  let segmentIndex = 0
  let cursor = 0

  while (cursor < stops.length - 1) {
    let endIndex = Math.min(cursor + waypointLimit + 1, stops.length - 1)
    let url = ""

    while (endIndex > cursor) {
      const waypoints = stops
        .slice(cursor + 1, endIndex)
        .map((stop) => stop.address)

      url = buildUrl(
        stops[cursor].address,
        stops[endIndex].address,
        waypoints
      )

      if (url.length <= MAX_URL_LENGTH) {
        break
      }

      endIndex -= 1
    }

    segments.push({
      label: `Segment ${segmentIndex + 1}`,
      url,
      stopCount: endIndex - cursor + 1,
    })

    segmentIndex += 1
    cursor = endIndex
  }

  return segments
}

```

Implemented a Medusa Admin “Trip Planner” page plus a `/admin/trip-planner/plan` backend endpoint, with Google Routes + Places API wrappers, itinerary timeline building, CSV export, and segmented Google Maps links. The UI is a single-page form that captures all required inputs and renders the itinerary, links, and CSV download, while the backend handles routing, optional stop discovery, and time window checks.

## File Tree Changes
- `src/admin/routes/trip-planner/page.tsx` (new)
